zh.lifeSummary: Sir Ambrosius Willis是Richard WYLLYS和Joane GRANT的兒子，出生在英國戴文普萊姆斯普萊姆斯 聖 安德魯。他和Agneta Coles至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1625年2月9日在英國瓦維其可爾分尼 康普頓。

nameMeanings.name: Willis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Will with genitival -s. This surname represents a retention of the second syllable, introduced by the addition of the Middle English genitival suffix -es, which would have been pronounced in the Middle English period. Compare Wills . In some cases the name is a variant of Willey , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Will
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Will, a short form of William . This surname is now commonly found in Scotland, in Aberdeenshire, Angus, and Kincardineshire.

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a spring or stream, from Middle English wel, wel(l)e, wille, in a southwestern dialect form. This surname is found predominantly in the south and southwestern parts of the country. Compare Well 1.
English: nickname from Middle English iwil, wil(l)e ‘agreeable, pleasing’.

nameMeanings.name: Ambrosius
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Dutch: from the Latin personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ), or a Latinized (humanistic) form of its German vernacular variant Ambros .

variants.name: Ambrus
meanings: Hungarian: from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name   Ambrosius   ‘Ambrose’. This surname is also common in Romania.

Slovak (also Ambrús, Ambruš, and Ambrúš); Slovenian and Croatian (Ambruš): from a Hungarian-influenced (see 1 above) regional variant of the personal name Slovak Ambróz, Slovenian and Croatian Ambrož ‘Ambrose’ (see Ambroz ).

variants.name: Ambros
meanings: form of the Late Latin name Ambrosius, from post-classical Greek Ambrosios ‘immortal’. This was borne by various early saints, most notably a 4th-century bishop of Milan. The name has never been common in England, but has enjoyed considerably greater popularity in Roman Catholic Ireland, where the surname Mac Ambrois is Anglicized as McCambridge.

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: form of the Late Latin name Ambrosius, from post-classical Greek Ambrosios ‘immortal’. This was borne by various early saints, most notably a 4th-century bishop of Milan. The name has never been common in England, but has enjoyed considerably greater popularity in Roman Catholic Ireland, where the surname Mac Ambrois is Anglicized as McCambridge.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Sir Ambrosius Willis was born in Plymouth St Andrew, Plymouth, Devon, England as the son of Richard WYLLYS and Joane GRANT. He had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Agneta Coles. He died on 9 February 1625, in Fenny Compton, Warwickshire, England.

ko.Plymouth St Andrew, Plymouth, Devon, Eng: 영국 데폰 플림우스 플림우스 스트 앤드루
